title: Anthropic releases Claude Opus 4.7 with 1M context window
text: Anthropic announced Claude Opus 4.7 with a one-million-token context window, enabling long-document analysis, full-codebase reasoning, and multi-hour transcript summarization in a single call. Pricing matches the 200k-context tier. Internal benchmarks show no degradation past 500k tokens, a known weak spot for prior long-context models.

title: Paper: speculative decoding cuts LLM inference cost by 3x at no quality loss
text: A new arXiv paper formalizes draft-and-verify speculative decoding for production LLM serving. Authors report 2.8x to 3.2x throughput gains across Llama and Mistral families with identical output distributions. Reference implementation runs on vLLM.

title: T-Mobile deploys LLM-powered call summaries in 1,200 retail stores
text: T-Mobile rolled out an AI assistant that drafts post-call summaries for customer-service reps in real time. Average call wrap-up dropped from 4 minutes to 50 seconds. The system runs on Anthropic Claude Haiku with a custom RAG layer over T-Mobile billing knowledge.

title: EU AI Act phase 2 enforcement begins June 1
text: The European Commission confirmed the second phase of AI Act enforcement starts June 1, 2026, targeting general-purpose AI systems above 10^25 FLOPs and requiring conformity assessments for high-risk deployments. Telecom carriers using AI for fraud detection or churn modeling may fall under the high-risk tier.

title: Open-source agent framework SmolAgents hits 1.0
text: Hugging Face shipped SmolAgents 1.0, a 2k-line Python framework for building tool-using LLM agents. Default backends include Anthropic, OpenAI, and any local model served via vLLM. Includes a sandboxed code-execution tool and a built-in tracer for debugging multi-step runs.

title: Vodafone Italy uses AI to predict network outages 4 hours in advance
text: Vodafone Italy reported its outage-prediction model now flags 87% of customer-impacting outages four hours before they occur, up from a 6-month baseline of 62%. The model ingests cell-tower telemetry, weather, and historical incident data. Avoided downtime is estimated at 1.4 million customer-hours per quarter.
